definitions.text: To delay movement or action until some event or time; to remain neglected or in readiness.
examples: Wait here until your car arrives.
I’m still waiting for you to pay me back the money I lent you..

definitions.text: To wait tables; to serve customers in a restaurant or other eating establishment.
examples: She used to wait in this joint.

definitions.text: To delay movement or action until the arrival or occurrence of; to await. (Now generally superseded by “wait for”.)

examples: I had a very long wait at the airport security check.
They lay in wait for the patrol.

definitions.text: Tells the other speaker to stop talking, typing etc. for a moment, often to allow clarification.
examples: - And so I went upstairs—- Wait. Your house has two floors?
